channel.title: Video Editor Jobs Blog
channel.description: A focused community and job board for video editors, post-production specialists, creator teams, agencies, and brands hiring editing talent.

item.title: How to Find Remote Video Editor Jobs Without Sorting Through Bad Gigs
item.description: Remote editing work is easiest to win when your proof matches the team's content format and your process makes collaboration feel low-risk.

item.title: Where to Find Video Editor Jobs: Boards, Communities, Creators, and Agencies
item.description: The best video editor opportunities are split across job boards, private communities, creator teams, agencies, referrals, and local production networks.

item.title: How to Find Video Editor Jobs: Search Terms, Communities, and Portfolio Proof
item.description: Finding better editing work is less about one job board and more about matching your search, portfolio, and outreach to the format teams need.

item.title: How to Get Jobs as a Video Editor: Portfolio, Outreach, and Proof
item.description: The easiest editor jobs to win are the ones where your portfolio, process, and outreach all match the work the hiring team already needs.

item.title: Video Editor Portfolio Guide: What Hiring Teams Need to See
item.description: A portfolio should make fit obvious. The strongest editor profiles show the exact kind of editing a hiring team needs next.

item.title: How to Hire a Video Editor: Brief, Budget, Workflow, and Portfolio Signals
item.description: The fastest way to hire a strong editor is to write a brief that filters for the actual workflow, not just taste.

item.title: Video Editor Job Description Template for Hiring Teams
item.description: Most editing job posts are too vague. This structure helps editors self-select before either side wastes time.

item.title: Freelance Video Editor Rates: How to Think About Budget and Scope
item.description: Rates make more sense when tied to deliverables, source footage, timeline, revision load, and the level of creative ownership required.

item.title: Video Editor Portfolio Examples: What to Show for Different Jobs
item.description: A strong portfolio is not one giant reel. It is a small set of matched examples that prove you can handle the exact editing workflow a team needs.

item.title: Video Editor Interview Questions for Hiring Teams
item.description: Good editor interviews test process as much as taste: how someone handles footage, notes, deadlines, revisions, and unclear creative direction.

item.title: YouTube Video Editor Job Description: What to Include
item.description: YouTube editor roles need more than software requirements. The post should explain channel format, pacing, cadence, references, analytics expectations, and review workflow.

item.title: How to Price Video Editing Work: Scope, Rates, and Revision Load
item.description: Pricing gets clearer when both sides define the real job: deliverables, footage volume, revision rounds, deadline pressure, and creative ownership.
